docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
Welcome to my website, which showcases the projects and houses documentation for the many scripts I've worked on. This itself is also a testament of my skill as a programmer and designer. This website serves as a more in-depth extension of my talent.roblox page.

The tabs on the side are self-explanatory. Anything in my portfolio is a showcase of my history and experience as a game-developer on Roblox. I'm currently trying to get verified on Roblox. Please follow me if you haven't already!

docs.title: About Me
docs.text: I'm an aspiring programmer with a knack for innovation and design. Game-development is a passion to me, and I aim to do this for a living. Ever since I was extremely young, I've always loved playing video games. The idea of being able to immerse yourself in a brand new world always came off as appealing to me. My love really sparked to life when I received my first console: The Nintendo GameCube. I experienced so many different feelings playing all of these different games. I loved every second of my experiences, and I occasionally go back to look at old games that brought me countless hours of joy. The idea that games can be a way to express yourself always dawned to me as the main draw to game-development as a whole. You may notice I've taken inspiration in the projects I've worked on, like my Untitled Platforming Game, or Observation Room. There's a list of projects I've worked on in the portfolio section.

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• My native language is English, but I can also speak Portuguese fluently because my family (on my mother's side) is entirely brazilian.
  • I'm currently studying at Full Sail University as a Software Engineer, and I plan to graduate with a Bachelor's Degree in October of 2025.
  • My time-zone is EST. It is currently ... for me.

docs.title: Skills
docs.text:
• I'm certified in AutoCAD, Fusion360, Autodesk Inventor, and Maya.
  • I can understand Spanish, and understand Chinese. I cannot speak Chinese fluently yet.
  • I know the following programming languages: C++, LUA, GML, PY, JS, and HTML5.
  • I have six years of experience using photoshop.
  • I have four years of experience using VEGAS Pro.
  • I can play both Guitar (Electric and Acoustic), and Piano.
  • I'm a semi-experienced digital artist.

docs.title: Strengths
docs.text:
• I am an extremely quick learner. I'm not an expert in JS or CSS, but I figured out how to make this site in two days.
  • I find helping others around me learn extremely gratifying, and I am happy to explain anything that may be confusing to someone.
  • I leave a lot of commentary on the scripts that I've created, regardless of documentation existing or not. The less confusion the better.
  • I take my career (regardless of job) very seriously and I pour as much as I can into what I do. I'm known as a hard worker by my peers around me.
  • I have very good cohesion with people on a team.
  • I can retain motivation and recover from burn-out extremely fast.

docs.title: Weaknesses
docs.text:
• I have a few hobbies. (This could also be a strength in some cases.) They are just enough to be noted as an actual weakness, but they are far from obstructive.
    • I practice my Guitar every week. (typically on Wed or Sat)
    • I study at Full-Sail University. (Mon-Fri)
    • I go to the gym frequently and typically at 5PM. I'm there for 1-2 hours. (Mon, Tue, Thur, Fri, Sat)
  • I may take a bit of time to settle into a new environment.
  • Occasionally I may feel periods of lethargy. Despite that, I'm very communicative about my state mentally.

docs.title: Why this website?
docs.text: I realized that the talent.roblox page doesn't do my experience justice, so I decided to create my own webpage on github to access all of my creations. I aim to use this website to host my portfolio and documentation for any kind of system I may (or have made) public for people to use. I rassled this together in the span of two days, so please excuse my occasional grammatical issues.

docs.title: CipherLib
docs.text: This is a library of modules I've created to expedite and provide more creative freedom to the user. Can be used to make loading and reading data from modules easy. You can access a module under it as such:

docs.title: Why use a loader?
docs.text: Because a loader can hold the data for the modules. It provides readability, and dynamically scales to the modules and folders below it. Because of the way it's structured, it's easy to add/remove your own custom modules.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
local loader = require(Path.To.Module)
local module = loader.module

-- If there is a folder, you do this instead:
local sub_module = loader.Folder.module

docs.title: How do I add/remove my own modules?
docs.text: It's as easy as dragging and dropping it underneath the loader. If you want the module to only be accessible on the client, add the ClientOnly tag to the module so that it may only be considered by the client when being loaded. The same works vice-versa with the ServerOnly tag. Adding them to folders will affect all of the modules underneath it, so keep that in mind!

docs.title: Default Modules
docs.text: Modules that come with the library at start.

docs.title: loader.SwitchCase
docs.text:
• function

    Emulates C++'s Switch Case in LuaU. Documentation is provided in the module itself.

docs.title: loader.YieldInstance
docs.text:
• function

    A unique version of WaitForChild(). Creates an instance if the server calls this method and does not find an instance of that name. Otherwise it will yield if it's on the client. Excellent for modules that are used by both the Client and the Server, and handle cross-server communication.

docs.title: CLIENT_UTILS.AnimationHandler
docs.text:
• AnimationHandler

    An animation handler I've designed that makes loading and adding animations effortless. Currently lacks documentation. This will be updated in the future.

docs.title: CLIENT_UTILS.EasyRig
docs.text:
• EasyRig

    An easy way to instantiate rigs into the workspace. Has its own methods to manage animations effortlessly.

docs.title: CLIENT_UTILS.CloneModel
docs.text:
• function

    Allows you to clone instances regardless of whether they're archivable or not.

docs.title: CLIENT_UTILS.CutsceneHandler
docs.text:
• function

    Handles Cutscenes that can manipulate your camera. Look under the "Cutscenes" folder for more info.

docs.title: CLIENT_UTILS.Icon
docs.text:
• Icon

    A modified version of the TopBar Plus module. Allows you to create an easily customizable top-bar at the top of your screen. Scales to any device.

docs.title: CLIENT_UTILS.Parallax
docs.text:
• Parallax

    A module that handles UI parallax scrolling. Can be customized to fit any speed and background. Changing the backgroundTransparency the Parallax is tied to will automatically adjust the transparency of the Parallax.

docs.title: CLIENT_UTILS.Typer
docs.text:
• Typer

    Allows you to easily create a typing animated effect to make text more visually interesting. Has two SmartSignals.

docs.title: CLIENT_UTILS.UI_SOUND
docs.text:
• UI_SOUND

    A module that allows you to easily reference and play/stop audio. Purely used from an organization standpoint.

docs.title: CLIENT_UTILS.ViewportModel
docs.text:
• ViewportModelClass

    A modified version of the ViewportModel class. Allows you to handle displaying models of any size in a viewport. Comes with a few methods to make things like manual rotation, and automatic rotation simple.

docs.title: DynaNPC
docs.text:
This is a dynamic NPC system that handles all of the brunt and heavy lifting. It will also handle garbage collection and memory de-allocation to save resources. Features numerous different signals, and dynamic type casting. All you need to do is provide it with a rig and it can do the rest!

Your browser does not support the video tag.

docs.title: Configs
docs.text:
These are properties that can be edited via NPC.Config, or instantiated in the NPC.new constructor in a table. Not including them in the table (or not giving a Config table at all) will leave them as the default values shown below: 

--// Debug Configs (DebugEnabled must be true to use any of the below)
DebugEnabled = true;
DB_LogConfigChanges = false; --// Logs changes to the config dictionary.
DB_LogStateOverwrites = false; --// Logs changes to the states dictionary.
DB_LogStateChanges = false; --// Logs changes to the NPC's states

--// Other stuff
WatchClosestPlayer = false; --// NPC will watch the closest player character to them.
AutoSpeed = false; --// A config value built into the Following state. The NPC character will speed up to catch up to a specific character.

--// Tools
CanEquipTools = false; --// Whether or not the NPC can hold onto tools.
CanPickupTools = false; --// Whether or not the NPC can pick up tools to begin with. (If they cannot equip a tool, but can pick up tools, they will store it in their backpack if they have one.)
HasBackpack = false; --// Whether or not the NPC has a backpack that can store items. Disabling this will cause the NPC to drop the items where they were picked up.

--// Destruction
DestroyRigWithModule = false; --// Whether calling the Destroy method on a NPC will destroy the rig it's tied to.
AutoDestroyOnDeath = false; --// Self-explanatory. The rig will automatically destroy on death.

--// Pathfinding
PathParams = {
    AgentCanClimb = true;
    AgentCanJump = true;

    WaypointSpacing = 4;
    Costs = {
        Avoid = math.huge;
}; --// Waypoint Parameters.

docs.title: NCParams
docs.text:
These are properties that can be passed to the GetNearestCharacter method of a NPC. Not including them in the table (or not giving a NCParams table at all) will leave them as the default values shown below: 

MustBeVisible = false; --// Must be visible to the NPC's CanSee method.
OnlyPlayerCharacters = true; --// Will only consider Player Characters in the method.
MustBeAlive = false; --// Ignores rigs without Humanoids, or rigs with health below 0.

Position = Vector3.new(); --// Defaults to the rig's pivot position. The position to get the closest character to.
MaxDistance = math.huge; --// The maximum distance a character can be before they are ignored by the method.

docs.title: DynaNPC.new(Rig, Configs?)
docs.text:
• Takes a Model and Configs table as parameters.

    Usage: 

    -- Modules
local DynaNPC = require(game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage").DynaNPC)

-- Instances
local Rig = game.workspace.Rig -- Can be any rig.

-- NPCs
local NPC = DynaNPC.new(Rig)
    Returns a NPC class, which can use the methods listed below.

docs.title: NPC:SetWatching(Part?)
docs.text:
• Takes a BasePart as a parameter.

    Returns a boolean indicating whether the NPC has successfully switched targets.

docs.title: NPC:CanSee(Target)
docs.text:
• Takes a BasePart or Model as a parameter.

    Returns a boolean indicating whether the NPC can see the model (any basepart under the model), or the target base part.

docs.title: NPC:SetState(NewState, ...)
docs.text:
• Takes a string and a tuple as parameters.

    Switches the NPC's state to the name if it exists, and runs any additional parameters on said function. Otherwise it'll switch to the None state. Passes the tuple to the LastParams table.

docs.title: NPC:GetNearestCharacter(NCParams?)
docs.text:
• Takes a NCParams table as a parameter.

    Gets the nearest character with parameters. (See above for the parameters.) May not return a character.

docs.title: NPC:GetBackpack()
docs.text:
• Does not have any parameters.

    Returns a NPC's backpack if it exists, or creates one (and returns it) if the NPC's HasBackpack config is enabled, and they don't have a backpack.

docs.title: NPC:GetTool(ToolName?)
docs.text:
• Takes a string as a parameter.

    Returns a tool in the NPC's backpack (if they have one), or the tool they're holding. Essentially FindFirstChildWhichIsA("Tool") but you can specify the name if you want.

docs.title: NPC:UseTool(ActivateTool?)
docs.text:
• Takes a boolean as a parameter.

    Activates the tool (or deactivates it if it's false), defaults to true. Does not return a value.

docs.title: NPC:DropTool(TargetTool)
docs.text:
• Takes a tool as a parameter.

    Drops the tool from the NPC's inventory or hand. Does not return a value.

docs.title: NPC:HandleCleanup(Object, AutoCleanup)
docs.text:
• Takes an instance, table, or thread as the first parameter. The second parameter is a boolean.

    Binds an object to be automatically cleaned up upon the NPC's destruction. If AutoCleanup is explicitly defined as false, then it'll remove it from the auto-cleanup if it's already bound.

  • If the parameter being passed is a table, the table must have a :Destroy() method, or it will not be automatically cleaned up.

docs.title: NPC:_DebugLog(LogText)
docs.text:
• Takes a string as a parameter.

    Logs text to the console. Will not log text if the NPC's DebugEnabled config is false.

docs.title: Events
docs.text: See SmartSignal for more info.

docs.title: NPC.StateUpdated
docs.text:
• Returns : string

    Fires whenever the NPC's state changes at any point.

docs.title: NPC.WaypointBegan
docs.text:
• Returns: PathWaypoint

    Fires whenever the NPC heads towards a waypoint.

docs.title: NPC.ChrTouched
docs.text:
• Returns: model

    Fires whenever the NPC touches a humanoid rig.

docs.title: NPC.Config.Changed
docs.text:
• Returns:Key, NewValue, OldValue

    See ProxyTable for more info.

docs.title: NPC.Events.Changed
docs.text:
• Returns: Key, NewValue, OldValue

    See ProxyTable for more info.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Properties of the NPC.

docs.title: NPC.Rig
docs.text:
• model

    The rig the NPC was instantiated with.

docs.title: NPC.Hum
docs.text:
• humanoid

    The humanoid of the rig the NPC was instantiated with.

docs.title: NPC._AutoCleanup
docs.text:
• table

    A table containing all of the objects to be cleaned up upon the NPC's destruction.

docs.title: NPC.Path
docs.text: A modified SimplePath module.

docs.title: NPC.AnimHandler
docs.text: An animation handler I've designed.

docs.title: NPC.Configs
docs.text:
• table

    Dictionary of values. Values can be overwritten directly and the Changed signal of this table will be fired.

docs.title: NPC.State
docs.text:
• string

    Stores the current state of the NPC.

docs.title: NPC.States
docs.text:
• table

    Dictionary of functions. Values can be overwritten directly and the Changed signal of this table will be fired.

docs.title: NPC.LastParams
docs.text:
• table

    Stores the last tuple passed by the NPC's SetState method.

docs.title: MusicHandler
docs.text:
This is a music player that handles the playback of music. Allows for complete control over traditional features that modern music players have, like Shuffle, Loop, and song indexing.

The music player in Observation Room.

Your browser does not support the video tag.

docs.title: Configs
docs.text:
These are properties that can be edited via MusicPlayer.Config These are the default values: 

AutoShuffle = false; --// Shuffles playlist on a new list automatically.
Crossfade = nil; --// If this is a number, when a song is this many seconds away from ending, it will gradually begin to fade into the next song.
Looping = false; --// If this is true, it will loop the song indefinitely.
AutoPlay = true; --// Automatically plays the next song after a song ends.
Sensitivity = 0.25; --// If you're using the visualizer add-on, this is the loudness the music player must reach to fire the visualizer effect.

docs.title: MusicHandler.new(Rig, Configs?)
docs.text:
• Takes an Instance as a parameter.

    Usage: 

    -- Modules
local MusicHandler = require(game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage").MusicHandler)

-- Instances
local PlayerGUI = game:GetService("Players").LocalPlayer.PlayerGUI

-- NPCs
local Player = MusicHandler.new(Rig)
    Creates a sound under the Instance, and returns a MusicPlayer class, which can use the methods listed below.

docs.title: Player:SetParent(NewParent?)
docs.text:
• Takes an Instance as a parameter.

    Moves the sound tied to this music player under the instance passed.

docs.title: Player:SetMusicGroup(NewGroup?)
docs.text:
• Takes a SoundGroup as a parameter.

    Switches the sound tied to this music player's sound group to the target group.

docs.title: Player:GetSongInfo()
docs.text:
• Does not have any parameters.

    Returns the song data stored in the Vars table.

docs.title: Player:Play()
docs.text:
• Does not have any parameters.

    Returns a boolean indicating whether the player could play/resume the current song.

docs.title: Player:Pause()
docs.text:
• Does not have any parameters.

    Pauses the song. The play method will resume the song at this position.

docs.title: Player:Stop()
docs.text:
• Does not have any parameters.

    Stops the song completely.

docs.title: Player:SetTimePosition(TimePosition?)
docs.text:
• Takes a number as a parameter.

    Jumps to a certain time position in a song. Defaults to 0.

docs.title: Player:SetIndex(NewIndex)
docs.text:
• Takes a number as a parameter.

    Jumps to a specific index in the music player's list. If the number is less than 1, it will automatically jump to the last song in the playlist. If the number is bigger than the playlist's length, it will jump to the first song in the playlist. Returns a boolean indicating whether the current song changed.

docs.title: Player:Next()
docs.text:
• Does not have any parameters.

    Moves onto the next song in the playlist.

docs.title: Player:Previous()
docs.text:
• Does not have any parameters.

    Moves back a song in the playlist.

docs.title: Player:Shuffle(MaintainIndex?)
docs.text:
• Takes a boolean as a parameter.

    Shuffles the playlist. If the parameter passed is true, it will not switch the song upon being shuffled.

docs.title: Player:Destroy()
docs.text:
• Does not have any parameters.

    Destroys the player and the sound tied to it, cleaning them up from memory.

docs.title: Events
docs.text: See SmartSignal for more info.

docs.title: Player.OnTick
docs.text:
• Returns : number

    Fires whenever the song's time position changes. Returns the remaining time of the song.

docs.title: Player.OnInfo
docs.text:
• Returns: ProductInfo

    See ProductInfo for more information.

docs.title: Player.OnBeat
docs.text:
• Returns: number

    Fires whenever the Music Player exceeds the music player's sensitivity config. Does not fire on server.

docs.title: Player.PlaylistEnded
docs.text:
• Does not return any values.

    Fires whenever the playlist ends or loops back to the first song.

docs.title: Player.Config.Changed
docs.text:
• Returns: Key, NewValue, OldValue

    See ProxyTable for more info.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Properties of the music player.

docs.title: Player.Sound
docs.text:
• Sound

    The sound tied to this music player. Destroying this will automatically clean up the music player.

docs.title: Player.Vars
docs.text:
• table

    Contains a table of variables the music player uses. 

    Playlist = {}; -- Playing songs.
DefaultPlaylist = {}; -- Default playlist.
Index = 1; -- Index of currently playing song in the table. (Roblox's array index starts at 1 instead of 0)
Playing = false; -- Determines if the playlist is currently playing a song or not.
Data = nil; -- Contains the song data.

docs.title: Player.Config
docs.text:
• ProxyTable

    A table containing the current configuration of the Music Player.

docs.title: ProxyTable
docs.text:
A module that allows you to easily create and manage Proxy Tables, an effective way to detect changes in tables.

Your browser does not support the video tag.

docs.title: ProxyManager.new(Data)
docs.text:
• Takes a table as a parameter. The table stores data in any way. It does not have to be a dictionary to detect changes.

    Usage: 

    -- Modules
local ProxyManager = require(game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage").ProxyManager)

local ProxyTable = ProxyManager.new({
    Name = "Cool Name";
    Health = 30;
    MaxHealth = 50;
})
    Returns a ProxyTable class, which can use the methods listed below.

docs.title: ProxyTable:Destroy()
docs.text:
• Does not have any parameters.

    Cleans up the Proxy Table from memory. All signals are automatically disconnected.

docs.title: Events
docs.text: See SmartSignal for more info.

docs.title: ProxyTable.Changed
docs.text:
• Returns: any, any, any

    Fires whenever the ProxyTable is changed. Returns the key, new value, and old value.

docs.title: SmartSignal
docs.text:
This is a system that handles bindable events, and allows you to type-cast to them so that you can allow Intellisense to fill-in the blanks. Reduce the amount of pain and frustration that may come with working with other people.

Your browser does not support the video tag.

docs.title: SmartSignal.new(...)
docs.text:
• Takes a tuple as a parameter. The tuple is not used for anything other than type-casting.

    Usage: 

    -- Modules
local SmartSignal = require(game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage").SmartSignal)

local Signal = SmartSignal.new()
    Returns a Signal class, which can use the methods listed below.

docs.title: Signal:Connect(callback)
docs.text:
• Takes a function as a parameter.

    Returns a RBXScriptSignal. The callback function will be fired whenever Signal:Fire() is called.

docs.title: Signal:ConnectParallel(callback)
docs.text:
• Takes a function as a parameter.

    Returns a RBXScriptSignal. The callback function will be fired whenever Signal:Fire() is called.

docs.title: Signal:Once(callback)
docs.text:
• Takes a function as a parameter.

    Returns a RBXScriptSignal. The callback function will be fired whenever Signal:Fire() is called.

docs.title: Signal:Wait(callback?)
docs.text:
• Takes a function as a parameter.

    If you passed a function, it will automatically run it and return its result once the event fires. Otherwise, it will return a tuple. Like Signal:Once(), but yields the script until the event fires.

docs.title: Signal:Fire(...)
docs.text:
• Takes a tuple as a parameter.

    Does not return a value. Fires the event.

docs.title: Signal:Destroy()
docs.text:
• Does not have any parameters.

    Cleans up the event from memory. All signals are automatically disconnected.

docs.title: WatchManager
docs.text:
This is a system that allows any rig to track and look at potential targets.

Your browser does not support the video tag.

docs.title: WatchManager.new(Rig)
docs.text:
• Takes a Model as a parameter. The Model has three requirements to function properly: It must have a BasePart named "Head", An Attachment named "NeckRigAttachment" or "FaceCenterAttachment", and have a PrimaryPart set.

    Usage: 

    -- Modules
local WatchManager = require(game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage").WatchManager)

local Rig = workspace.Rig
local Watcher = WatchManager.new(Rig)
    Returns a Watcher class, which can use the methods listed below.

docs.title: Watcher:SetLooking(Part)
docs.text:
• Takes a BasePart as a parameter.

    Does not return a value. The rig will look at the BasePart. If the Rig was not looking at anything before, it will gradually look over at the target.

docs.title: Watcher:CanSee(Target)
docs.text:
• Takes a BasePart or Model as a parameter.

    Returns a boolean indicating whether the Rig can see the target from the Rig's PrimaryPart.

docs.title: Watcher:GetNearestCharacter(NCParams)
docs.text:
• See NCParams for more info.

    Gets the nearest character with parameters. (See above for the parameters.) May not return a character.

docs.title: Watcher:TweenWeight(TargetWeight, tweenInfo?)
docs.text:
• Takes a number and TweenInfo as parameters.

    The number cannot be less than 0, or greater than 1. Tweens the weight of the IK on the rig. 1 having complete control over the rig, while 0 has no control.

docs.title: Watcher:AutoCleanup(Object, AutoCleanup)
docs.text:
• See HandleCleanup for more info.
  • Takes an instance, table, or thread as the first parameter. The second parameter is a boolean.

    Binds an object to be automatically cleaned up upon the NPC's destruction. If AutoCleanup is explicitly defined as false, then it'll remove it from the auto-cleanup if it's already bound.

  • If the parameter being passed is a table, the table must have a :Destroy() method, or it will not be automatically cleaned up.

docs.title: Watcher:Destroy()
docs.text:
• Does not have any parameters.

    Cleans up the watcher and deletes the IK from memory. This is automatically called if the IK created by this class, or the NPC tied to this class are destroyed.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: A group I worked for. Owned by @Eladerin.

docs.title: Cliffside Academy
docs.text:
A roleplaying game based on the popular Rockstar game, Bully.

You can look at the game here.

docs.title: My contribution
docs.text: I handled smaller bug-fixes and I programmed tools to facilitate roleplay immersion for this game. I also did role tags for staff and other important figures.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: A group I've founded with @Stew_Fox. We collaborate on two small but growing projects and plan on releasing them in the future.

docs.title: Observation Room
docs.text:
An adventure/vibe game underneath Frost Fox Studio. The main draw to this game is the large open environment. I am responsible for all of the programming and UI design.

Has many features that are either upcoming, or complete. Including but not limited to:

  • A unique loading and title screen.
  • A music player and visualizer. (Cave crystals pulse to the music.)
  • Regional Music, Ambience, and Lighting.
  • Numerous NPC vendors with their own shops and unique dialogue.
  • An emote wheel.
  • Animated seats players can sit on.
  • A dynamic settings system.
  • A flexible lighting system that utilizes CollectionService to promote organization.
  • Unique tools that add different ways to experience the game.
  • Custom prompts that allow for more unique interactions without sacrificing visibility.

You can look at the game here.

docs.title: My contribution
docs.text: I'm responsible for all of the programming, and all of the UI work. You can see my impact in the credits of this game on the main menu.

docs.title: Media
docs.text: Your browser does not support the video tag.

docs.title: Virulence
docs.text:
An open-world survival infection survival game. Has many features that are either upcoming, or complete. Including but not limited to:

  • A sickness system with amputation (cartoony, not realistic).
  • Augmented limbs to replace the ones you've lost.
  • A fully functional OTS gun system.
  • A dynamic music player that updates based on what's going on.
  • An inventory system that allows you to customize each division of a group.
  • A deployment system that can be customized to allow specific players to spawn in some locations.
  • A dynamic weather system that uses raycasts to remain accurate regardless of your location.
  • A voice-line system that allows users (and NPC characters) to be more expressive.

You can look at the game here.

docs.title: My contribution
docs.text: I'm responsible for the majority of the programming, and all of the UI work. All of the systems above were programmed by me. You can see my impact in the credits of this game on the main menu.

docs.title: Media
docs.text: Your browser does not support the video tag.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: A group I've done commissions for. Owned by @JustLikeB3nny.

docs.title: My contribution
docs.text: I'm responsible for the game's design model. I designed the Locations, Story, Characters, Gameplay Loop, Monetization Model, and Mechanics. I purely worked on this game as a game designer, so I did not do any programming whatsoever.

docs.title: Untitled Platforming Game
docs.text:
A platforming game akin to Celeste, but in a 3D environment. I created this game in one day.

You can look at the game here.

docs.title: My contribution
docs.text: I've done all of the UI, programming, and map-design. The animations were provided by @Bravo_RBXL.
